prevalence.trend: increasing recognition with urbanization; underdiagnosis persists

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; suicide risk and comorbidity effects

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11 criteria; somatic presentations common; exclude bipolar disorder and medical mimics

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Low to moderate due to limited access to psychotherapy and specialist care

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Primary care detection is variable; diagnosis often delayed; services concentrated in tertiary centers
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SSRIs and counseling/CBT where available; telepsychiatry and community programs expanding under NMHP
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High stigma, shortage of professionals, and out-of-pocket costs
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Affordable generic antidepressants and expanding national mental health initiatives
